agent_report.question: Salah's fixtures look tough — should I bench him this week?
agent_report.analysis: Salah's aggregate fixture difficulty rating sits at 3.33, which falls in the amber zone and does support some concern, but it is not a clear red-light scenario. More worrying is his current FPL form score of just 0.5 — a dramatic decline from his season average of 4.4 points per game — indicating he has been blanking in recent gameweeks. He has zero injury risk, so the dip is purely form-based rather than fitness-related, and his underlying season numbers (5 goals, 6 assists, 97 pts) show he remains a world-class asset. However, in your squad Salah is not captained (B.Fernandes holds the armband at 2×), so the cost of a bad Salah week is lower than it would be for a captain. Crucially, you have Rice (£7.3m) and Semenyo (£8.2m) as midfield bench options — both carry lower ceilings and neither realistically replaces Salah's upside even in amber fixtures. Benching a £14.0m midfielder averaging 4.4 ppg over the season for a short-term form blip, especially when you are not captaining him, is rarely the right call.

players.verdict: Elite season-long output (97 pts, 11 G/A) but recent form has collapsed to 0.5. Amber fixtures and a non-captain role reduce the downside risk, but he should still start given the lack of viable bench replacements.

agent_report.recommendation: Keep Salah in your starting XI — his season-long quality and non-captain role in your team mean the risk of benching him far outweighs the reward; ride out the form dip.
caveats: Fixture difficulty is a single aggregate score (3.33); per-GW breakdown for GW10 specifically is not available, so the exact opponent is unconfirmed.
Form score of 0.5 reflects a recent window — the exact number of games in that window is not specified, so severity of the dip is approximate.
Injury data was last updated 2026-04-19; confirm no late fitness concerns closer to the deadline.
Semenyo is listed under Man City in squad data but plays for Bournemouth — this appears to be a data labelling issue and does not affect the Salah analysis.
